title: Don’t put your name to bot-written content
content_html:
I realised today that this:

… is the vibe I get when people put their name and face to AI-generated output, and publish it as if it’s their own work. Any 80s kids like me will recognise that as Microsoft Word 97, which was made 30 years ago this year, and came with WordArt functions that mesmerised a generation who were instantly convinced that they were design experts. Of course, we were wrong and graphic design didn’t disappear as a discipline overnight. Word 97 is long discontinued and defunct,Though I think still much-loved for its simplicity but I wondered whether maybe it has some lessons to teach us?

I think it does, and first among them that even if something feels like it gave you a superpower, that new magic may not be as good as it seems at all. Worse still when everyone has it — the quirky rainbow art from 1996 faded to banality very quickly when all your friends’ school projects end up looking the same.

Second - perhaps sometimes the most obvious way to use a new tool isn’t the best way to use it? Just because you can now make your words pop, shine, and wobble with a few clicks doesn’t mean you should. Just because an AI can now create your social media “content” quickly and more easily without the need for any thought doesn’t mean that you should: it’s not so fun for your audience to read. I’ve benefitted from AI in so many ways - learning all sorts of things, finding mistakes, actually-useful research, vibe-coding quick tools and scripts. But asking it to generate writing to post as if I created it myself is not one of them, because it breaks the social contract of writing.Johanna Larsson is another Londoner who values AI but recognises its limits, and her blog is well worth reading.

The people that followed you aren’t reading your words any more, and your name and face has stopped being a guarantee of origin.

Finally, there are all sorts of things that software can do to take away the inhumanly boring tasks, improve our access to information and our ability to interface with the digital world. I love that I can find new information, link learning topics in ways I would never have been able to do before, and save time checking my spelling. Using it for all those things is great, but I’ll always pause before asking it to do the human things which make us human. Express yourself - don’t ask the AI to express yourself for you.

Microsoft got it right here too: their tagline encouraged you to "unleash the power of your words", giving you time to "focus on your content".

Speed isn’t everything. Some of the best ideas come when we’re forced to pause.

Image Credits

Clippit via Microsoft and Wikimedia
CD photograph via CD-Rom Preservation Project

Any 80s kids like me will recognise that as Microsoft Word 97, which was made 30 years ago this year, and came with WordArt functions that mesmerised a generation who were instantly convinced that they were design experts. Of course, we were wrong and graphic design didn’t disappear as a discipline overnight. Word 97 is long discontinued and defunct,Though I think still much-loved for its simplicity but I wondered whether maybe it has some lessons to teach us? I think it does, and first among them that even if something feels like it gave you a superpower, that new magic may not be as good as it seems at all. Worse still when everyone has it — the quirky rainbow art from 1996 faded to banality very quickly when all your friends’ school projects end up looking the same. Just because you can now make your words pop, shine, and wobble with a few clicks doesn’t mean you should. Just because an AI can now create your social media “content” quickly and more easily without the need for any thought doesn’t mean that you should: it’s not so fun for your audience to read. Microsoft got it right here too: their tagline encouraged you to "unleash the power of your words", giving you time to "focus on your content". ⊕ Even Clippit didn’t try to take away the human part of letter writing. Speed isn’t everything. Some of the best ideas come when we’re forced to pause. Image Credits Clippit via Microsoft and Wikimedia CD photograph via CD-Rom Preservation Project

sentences.en: I realised today that this:
sentences.zh: 我今天意识到：

blocks.html_en: … is the vibe I get when people put their name and face to AI-generated output, and publish it as if it’s their own work. Any 80s kids like me will recognise that as Microsoft Word 97, which was made 30 years ago this year, and came with WordArt functions that mesmerised a generation who were instantly convinced that they were design experts. Of course, we were wrong and graphic design didn’t disappear as a discipline overnight. Word 97 is long discontinued and defunct,Though I think still much-loved for its simplicity but I wondered whether maybe it has some lessons to teach us?

sentences.en: is the vibe I get when people put their name and face to AI-generated output, and publish it as if it’s their own work.
sentences.zh: 当人们将自己的名字和面孔输入到人工智能生成的输出中，并将其发布为自己的作品时，我感受到的就是这样的感觉。

sentences.en: Any 80s kids like me will recognise that as Microsoft Word 97, which was made 30 years ago this year, and came with WordArt functions that mesmerised a generation who were instantly convinced that they were design experts.
sentences.zh: 像我这样的 80 后孩子都会认出 Microsoft Word 97，它是 30 年前的今年推出的，它的艺术字功能让一代人着迷，他们立即确信自己是设计专家。

sentences.en: Of course, we were wrong and graphic design didn’t disappear as a discipline overnight.
sentences.zh: 当然，我们错了，平面设计作为一门学科并没有在一夜之间消失。

sentences.en: Word 97 is long discontinued and defunct,Though I think still much-loved for its simplicity but I wondered whether maybe it has some lessons to teach us?
sentences.zh: Word 97 早已停产并已不复存在，虽然我认为它的简单性仍然深受喜爱，但我想知道它是否可以教给我们一些教训？

blocks.html_en: I think it does, and first among them that even if something feels like it gave you a superpower, that new magic may not be as good as it seems at all. Worse still when everyone has it — the quirky rainbow art from 1996 faded to banality very quickly when all your friends’ school projects end up looking the same.

sentences.en: I think it does, and first among them that even if something feels like it gave you a superpower, that new magic may not be as good as it seems at all.
sentences.zh: 我认为确实如此，首先，即使某些东西感觉像是给了你超能力，但新魔法可能根本不像看起来那么好。

sentences.en: Worse still when everyone has it — the quirky rainbow art from 1996 faded to banality very quickly when all your friends’ school projects end up looking the same.
sentences.zh: 更糟糕的是，当每个人都拥有它时 - 当你所有朋友的学校项目最终看起来都一样时，1996 年的古怪彩虹艺术很快就变得平庸了。

blocks.html_en: Second - perhaps sometimes the most obvious way to use a new tool isn’t the best way to use it? Just because you can now make your words pop, shine, and wobble with a few clicks doesn’t mean you should. Just because an AI can now create your social media “content” quickly and more easily without the need for any thought doesn’t mean that you should: it’s not so fun for your audience to read. I’ve benefitted from AI in so many ways - learning all sorts of things, finding mistakes, actually-useful research, vibe-coding quick tools and scripts. But asking it to generate writing to post as if I created it myself is not one of them, because it breaks the social contract of writing.Johanna Larsson is another Londoner who values AI but recognises its limits, and her blog is well worth reading.

sentences.en: Second - perhaps sometimes the most obvious way to use a new tool isn’t the best way to use it?
sentences.zh: 其次，也许有时使用新工具的最明显的方法并不是最好的使用方法？

sentences.en: Just because you can now make your words pop, shine, and wobble with a few clicks doesn’t mean you should.
sentences.zh: 仅仅因为您现在只需点击几下鼠标就可以让您的文字变得流行、闪亮和摇摆，但这并不意味着您应该这样做。

sentences.en: Just because an AI can now create your social media “content” quickly and more easily without the need for any thought doesn’t mean that you should: it’s not so fun for your audience to read.
sentences.zh: 仅仅因为人工智能现在可以快速、更轻松地创建你的社交媒体“内容”而不需要任何思考，并不意味着你应该这样做：这对你的读者来说阅读起来并不那么有趣。

sentences.en: I’ve benefitted from AI in so many ways - learning all sorts of things, finding mistakes, actually-useful research, vibe-coding quick tools and scripts.
sentences.zh: 我在很多方面都从人工智能中受益——学习各种各样的东西、发现错误、实际有用的研究、振动编码快速工具和脚本。

sentences.en: But asking it to generate writing to post as if I created it myself is not one of them, because it breaks the social contract of writing.Johanna Larsson is another Londoner who values AI but recognises its limits, and her blog is well worth reading.
sentences.zh: 但要求它像我自己创建一样生成帖子并不是其中之一，因为它打破了写作的社会契约。约翰娜·拉尔森（Johanna Larsson）是另一位重视人工智能但认识到其局限性的伦敦人，她的博客非常值得一读。

blocks.html_en: The people that followed you aren’t reading your words any more, and your name and face has stopped being a guarantee of origin.

sentences.en: The people that followed you aren’t reading your words any more, and your name and face has stopped being a guarantee of origin.
sentences.zh: 跟随你的人不再阅读你的话，你的名字和面孔也不再是出身的保证。

blocks.html_en: Finally, there are all sorts of things that software can do to take away the inhumanly boring tasks, improve our access to information and our ability to interface with the digital world. I love that I can find new information, link learning topics in ways I would never have been able to do before, and save time checking my spelling. Using it for all those things is great, but I’ll always pause before asking it to do the human things which make us human. Express yourself - don’t ask the AI to express yourself for you.

sentences.en: Finally, there are all sorts of things that software can do to take away the inhumanly boring tasks, improve our access to information and our ability to interface with the digital world.
sentences.zh: 最后，软件可以做各种各样的事情来消除非人性的无聊任务，改善我们获取信息的方式以及我们与数字世界交互的能力。

sentences.en: I love that I can find new information, link learning topics in ways I would never have been able to do before, and save time checking my spelling.
sentences.zh: 我喜欢我可以找到新信息，以我以前从未做过的方式链接学习主题，并节省检查拼写的时间。

sentences.en: Using it for all those things is great, but I’ll always pause before asking it to do the human things which make us human.
sentences.zh: 将它用于所有这些事情很棒，但在要求它做使我们成为人类的人类事情之前我总是会停下来。

sentences.en: Express yourself - don’t ask the AI to express yourself for you.
sentences.zh: 表达自己——不要要求人工智能为你表达自己。

blocks.html_en: Microsoft got it right here too: their tagline encouraged you to "unleash the power of your words", giving you time to "focus on your content".

sentences.en: Microsoft got it right here too: their tagline encouraged you to "unleash the power of your words", giving you time to "focus on your content".
sentences.zh: 微软在这里也做到了这一点：他们的口号鼓励你“释放言语的力量”，让你有时间“专注于你的内容”。

blocks.html_en: Speed isn’t everything. Some of the best ideas come when we’re forced to pause.

sentences.en: Speed isn’t everything.
sentences.zh: 速度并不是一切。

sentences.en: Some of the best ideas come when we’re forced to pause.
sentences.zh: 当我们被迫停下来时，一些最好的想法就会出现。

sentences.zh: 图片来源

sentences.zh: Clippit 来自 Microsoft 和 Wikimedia CD 照片来自 CD-Rom Preservation Project

content_html_zh:
我今天意识到：

… 当人们将自己的名字和面孔输入到人工智能生成的输出中，并将其发布为自己的作品时，我感受到的就是这样的感觉。 像我这样的 80 后孩子都会认出 Microsoft Word 97，它是 30 年前的今年推出的，它的艺术字功能让一代人着迷，他们立即确信自己是设计专家。 当然，我们错了，平面设计作为一门学科并没有在一夜之间消失。 Word 97 早已停产并已不复存在，虽然我认为它的简单性仍然深受喜爱，但我想知道它是否可以教给我们一些教训？

我认为确实如此，首先，即使某些东西感觉像是给了你超能力，但新魔法可能根本不像看起来那么好。 更糟糕的是，当每个人都拥有它时 - 当你所有朋友的学校项目最终看起来都一样时，1996 年的古怪彩虹艺术很快就变得平庸了。

其次，也许有时使用新工具的最明显的方法并不是最好的使用方法？ 仅仅因为您现在只需点击几下鼠标就可以让您的文字变得流行、闪亮和摇摆，但这并不意味着您应该这样做。 仅仅因为人工智能现在可以快速、更轻松地创建你的社交媒体“内容”而不需要任何思考，并不意味着你应该这样做：这对你的读者来说阅读起来并不那么有趣。 我在很多方面都从人工智能中受益——学习各种各样的东西、发现错误、实际有用的研究、振动编码快速工具和脚本。 但要求它像我自己创建一样生成帖子并不是其中之一，因为它打破了写作的社会契约。约翰娜·拉尔森（Johanna Larsson）是另一位重视人工智能但认识到其局限性的伦敦人，她的博客非常值得一读。

跟随你的人不再阅读你的话，你的名字和面孔也不再是出身的保证。

最后，软件可以做各种各样的事情来消除非人性的无聊任务，改善我们获取信息的方式以及我们与数字世界交互的能力。 我喜欢我可以找到新信息，以我以前从未做过的方式链接学习主题，并节省检查拼写的时间。 将它用于所有这些事情很棒，但在要求它做使我们成为人类的人类事情之前我总是会停下来。 表达自己——不要要求人工智能为你表达自己。

微软在这里也做到了这一点：他们的口号鼓励你“释放言语的力量”，让你有时间“专注于你的内容”。

速度并不是一切。 当我们被迫停下来时，一些最好的想法就会出现。

图片来源

Clippit 来自 Microsoft 和 Wikimedia CD 照片来自 CD-Rom Preservation Project
